hadiths.chapter: 22. THE RITUAL BATH OF JUMU’AH, ENTERING THE BATHHOUSE, ITS ETIQUETTE, AND TEACHINGS ON
hadiths.ar:
282 - وَّق َّال عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ: ق ِى  ق َّوْلِ إَّلل َّهِ ي َّعَّالَّى ۝ وَّ إ َّعِذ ُّوإ لَّهُّمْ مَّا إِسْي َّطَّعْي ُّمْ مِن ْ ق ُّو َّة ٍ  ۝» ق َّال 
ن ِالس َّوَّإذِ وَّ إ ِن  َّ رَّج ُّلاً ذَّج َّلَّ عَّلَّى رَّسُّولِ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ وَّ ق َّذْ صَّق  َّزَّ لِحْي َّي َّهُّ ق َّق َّ الَّ لَّهُّ رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ 
عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ عْذَّ هَّذ َّإ وَّ ق َّذْ إ َّق ْن َّى ن ِالْحِن  َّاءِ ق َّي َّن َّس َّمَّ رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ 
وَّق َّالَّ  «إ َّجْسَّن ُّ مِن ْ ذ َّإك   
وَّ ذ َّإك .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} said: "Dyeing (the hair or beard) with 
black brings comfort to women and inspires awe in enemies."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Kafi, Volume 6, p. 483 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, 
Vol. 1, p. 122 • Makarim Al -Akhlaq, vol. 1, p. 80 • Awaali al -La’aali, Part 4, p. 14 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 642 • 
Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 2, p. 89 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 73, p. 100 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 22, H. 281

hadiths.chapter: 22. THE RITUAL BATH OF JUMU’AH, ENTERING THE BATHHOUSE, ITS ETIQUETTE, AND TEACHINGS ON
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} commented on the verse of Allah {SWT}, 
the Exalted, "And prepare for them whatever force you can muster"  
(Surah Al-Anfal; 8:60). 
Imam {a.s} said: "Among it is dyeing with black”.  
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} narrated;  
“A man entered upon the Messenger of Allah {saws} with his beard dyed yellow (light 
color), and the Messenger of Allah {saws} said to him, "How good this is."  
Then he entered again after that with his beard dyed with henna (reddish color), and 
the Messenger of Allah {saws} smiled and said: "This is better than the previous."  
Then he entered again after having dyed it black, and the Prophet smiled and said:  
"This is better than both of the previous”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 123 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 643

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 22, H. 282

hadiths.chapter: 22. THE RITUAL BATH OF JUMU’AH, ENTERING THE BATHHOUSE, ITS ETIQUETTE, AND TEACHINGS ON
hadiths.ar:
284   -  وَّق َّال إ َّن ُّو ج َّعْق َّزٍ إَّلْن َّاق ِزُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «
ق َّلاَّ ن َّا ْسَّ ن ِي َّع ْي ِي زِهَّا .  
وق ذ ج ص ث  إلأ ن مه  علي هم إلسلام ن الوسمه ،  وإلح ص اب  ن الصق زة  ج ص اب  إلأي مان ، وإلأق ن اء   ج ص اب  إلأسلام ون السوإذ إ سلام  
وإ ي مان  ون ور.
hadiths.en:
Imam Al-Sadiq {a.s} said: "A woman should not neglect herself, even if it means 
wearing a necklace around her neck, Nor should She leave her hands without dyeing, 
even if it is by lightly applying henna, even if She is elderly."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Kafi, Volume 5, p. 509 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, 
Vol. 1, p. 123 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 645 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 22, p. 858 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’a, vol. 2, p. 97 • Wasa’il Al -
Shi’a, vol. 4, p. 459 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 20, p. 166 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 22, H. 283

hadiths.chapter: 22. THE RITUAL BATH OF JUMU’AH, ENTERING THE BATHHOUSE, ITS ETIQUETTE, AND TEACHINGS ON
hadiths.ar:
285  -  وَّق َّال رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ لِعَّلِى ٍّ  عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «
ذِرْهَّمٍ ق ِى  ع َّي ْزِةِ ق ِى  سَّن ِي لِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ وَّ ق ِي هِ إ َّرْن َّغَّ عَّس ْزَّة َّ ج َّصْلَّه ً ي َّطْزُّذُّ إَّلزِّي حَّ مِن َّ إَّلْأ ُّذ ن َّي ْن ِ وَّ ي َّح ْلُّو إَّلْن َّصَّزَّ وَّ ي ُّلَّي ِّن ُّ 
إَّلْح َّي َّاس ِي مَّ وَّ ي ُّطَّي ِّث ُّ  إَّلن  َّكْهَّه َّ وَّ ي َّس ُّذ ُّ إَّللِّي َّه َّ وَّ ي َّذ ْهَّث ُّ ن ِالص  َّن َّى وَّ ي ُّق ِل ُّ وَّسْوَّسَّه َّ إَّلس  َّي ْطَّان ِ وَّ ي َّق ْزَّحُّ ن ِهِ إَّلْمَّلاَّن ِكَّه ُّ  وَّ ي َّسْي َّن ْس ِزُّ 
ن ِهِ إَّلْمُّو ْمِن ُّ وَّ ي َّع ِي ظ ُّ ن ِهِ إَّلْكَّاق ِزَّ وَّ هُّوَّ ر ِي ن َّه   وَّ طِي ث   وَّ ي َّسْي َّحِى  مِن ْهُّ مُّن ْكَّز  وَّ ن َّكِي ز  وَّ  هُّوَّ ن َّزَّإءَّة   لَّهُّ ق ِى  ق َّن ْزِةِ .
hadiths.en:
Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} said:  
"When nails are affected by Nuura (depilatory paste), they can change their 
appearance, such that they may resemble the nails of the deceased. Therefore, there 
is no harm in changing (their appearance)." 
[AL SADUQ] 
The Imams have used wasmah (a natural dye), and dyeing with yellow (or light colors) 
is considered the dye of faith. Dyeing dark is the dye of Islam, and dyeing with black is 
both Islam, faith, and light. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 123 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 645 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 2, p. 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 22, H. 284

hadiths.chapter: 22. THE RITUAL BATH OF JUMU’AH, ENTERING THE BATHHOUSE, ITS ETIQUETTE, AND TEACHINGS ON
hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {saws} said to Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s}:  
"O’ Ali, spending a dirham on dyeing is better than spending a thousand dirhams in 
other paths for the sake of Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Glorious. It has fourteen benefits: 
it repels odors from the ears, improves eyesight, softens the nostrils, freshens breath, 
strengthens the gums, removes ailments, reduces the whispering of Satan, brings joy

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 22, H. 285

hadiths.chapter: 22. THE RITUAL BATH OF JUMU’AH, ENTERING THE BATHHOUSE, ITS ETIQUETTE, AND TEACHINGS ON
hadiths.ar: 287 - وَّق َّال رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ لِزَّج ُّلٍ .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"I indeed shave (the head) every Jumu’ah, from one washing to the next."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Kafi, Volume 6, p. 485 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, 
Vol. 1, p. 124 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 648 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 2, p. 107 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 22, H. 286

hadiths.chapter: 22. THE RITUAL BATH OF JUMU’AH, ENTERING THE BATHHOUSE, ITS ETIQUETTE, AND TEACHINGS ON
hadiths.ar:
288 - وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  « . 
وَّ مَّعْن َّى هَّذ َّإ ق ِى  ق َّوْلِ إَّلن  َّن ِى ِّ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ :  جِي ن َّ وَّصَّف َّ إَّلْح َّوَّإرِح َّ ق َّق َّال 
إَّلس َّهْمُّ مِن َّ إَّلز َّمِي  َّه ِ وَّ عَّلاَّمَّي ُّهُّمُّ إَّلي  َّسْن ِي ذُّ .
hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {saws} said to a man:  
"Shave (the head) for it enhances your beauty." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 22, H. 287

hadiths.chapter: 22. THE RITUAL BATH OF JUMU’AH, ENTERING THE BATHHOUSE, ITS ETIQUETTE, AND TEACHINGS ON
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"Shaving the head outside of Hajj or 'Umrah is a form of humiliation for your enemies 
and a source of beauty for you." 
The meaning of these words the Prophet became apparent, when He described the 
Khawarij, saying:  
"They will exit from the religion as an arrow exits from the bow, and their sign is shaving 
(the head) completely. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 124 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2, p. 523 • Al -
Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 649 • Al-Wasail al-Shia, Vol. 2, p. 106 • Al-Wasail al-Shia vol. 14 p. 232

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 22, H. 288

hadiths.chapter: 22. THE RITUAL BATH OF JUMU’AH, ENTERING THE BATHHOUSE, ITS ETIQUETTE, AND TEACHINGS ON
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"Removing hair from the nose enhances the appearance of the face."  
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Volume 6, p. 488 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 124 • Makarim Al -Akhlaq, 
vol. 1, p. 59 • Awaali al-La’aali, Part 4, p. 14 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 660 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 2, p. 118 • Bihar 
Al Anwar, Vol. 73, p. 91 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 73, p. 109 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 22, H. 289

reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٢، ح ٢٨٩

hadiths.chapter: 22. THE RITUAL BATH OF JUMU’AH, ENTERING THE BATHHOUSE, ITS ETIQUETTE, AND TEACHINGS ON
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"Washing the head with Al -khatmi (a type of herbal shampoo or a plant known as 
marshmallow root) every Jumu’ah provides protection from vitiligo and insanity."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al -Kafi, Volume 3, p. 418 • Al -Kafi, Volume 6, p. 504 • 
Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 124 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 3, p. 236 • Al -Waezin, volume 2, page 
307 • Makarim Al-Akhlaq, vol. 1, p. 60 • Jamal al -Usbu, Part 1, p. 362 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 631 • Wasa’il Al -
Shi’a, vol. 7, p. 354 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 73, p. 87 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 22, H. 290

hadiths.chapter: 22. THE RITUAL BATH OF JUMU’AH, ENTERING THE BATHHOUSE, ITS ETIQUETTE, AND TEACHINGS ON
hadiths.ar: 292 - وَّ ق ِى  ج َّن َّزٍ إ ج َّز ق َّالَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم .
hadiths.en:
(Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} ) states:  
"Washing the head with Al -khatmi (a type of herbal shampoo or marshmallow root) 
removes poverty and increases sustenance." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 124 • Al -Ādāb al-Dīniyyah, Part 1, p. 63 • Makarim Al -
Akhlaq, vol. 1, p. 60 • Awaali al-La’aali, Part 4, p. 13 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 632 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 22, H. 291

hadiths.chapter: 22. THE RITUAL BATH OF JUMU’AH, ENTERING THE BATHHOUSE, ITS ETIQUETTE, AND TEACHINGS ON
hadiths.en:
In another report, (Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} ) states: 
"Washing the head with Al-khatmi (a type of herbal shampoo or marshmallow root) is 
a remedy and a form of refreshment”. 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Volume 6, p. 504 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 124 • Makarim Al -Akhlaq, 
vol. 1, p. 60 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 632 • Wasa’il Al-Shia, vol. 2, p. 61

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 22, H. 292

reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٢، ح ٢٩٢

hadiths.chapter: 22. THE RITUAL BATH OF JUMU’AH, ENTERING THE BATHHOUSE, ITS ETIQUETTE, AND TEACHINGS ON
hadiths.ar:
294   -  وَّ إ ِن  َّ رَّسُّولَّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ إِع ْي َّم َّ ق َّا َّمَّزَّةُّ ج َّن ْزَّن ِي لُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن ْ ي َّع ْسِلَّ رَّإ ْسَّهُّ ن ِالسِّذْرِ وَّ كَّان َّ ذ َّلِك َّ  
سِذْرإً مِن ْ سِذْرَّة ِ إَّلْمُّن ْي َّهَّى .
hadiths.en:
Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali Ibn Abi Talib {a.s} said:  
"Washing the head with Al -khatmi (a type of herbal shampoo or marshmallow root) 
removes dirt and purifies impurities”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 22, H. 293

hadiths.chapter: 22. THE RITUAL BATH OF JUMU’AH, ENTERING THE BATHHOUSE, ITS ETIQUETTE, AND TEACHINGS ON
hadiths.en:
And when the Messenger of Allah {saws} was distressed, Archangel Jibril {a.s} 
instructed him to wash his head with sidr (lote tree leaves), and this sidr was from the 
lote tree of Sidrat al-Muntaha (the utmost boundary in the heavens)." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 125 • Al-Wafi, V. 6, p. 633 • Wasa’il Al-Shia, v. 2, p. 63 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 22, H. 294

hadiths.chapter: 22. THE RITUAL BATH OF JUMU’AH, ENTERING THE BATHHOUSE, ITS ETIQUETTE, AND TEACHINGS ON
hadiths.ar:
296  -  وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «
وَّ مَّن ْ ع َّسَّلَّ رَّإ ْسَّهُّ ن ِوَّرَّق ِ إَّلسِّذْرِ صَّزَّف َّ إَّلل َّهُّ عَّن ْهُّ وَّسْوَّسَّه َّ إَّلس  َّي ْطَّان ِ سَّن ْعِي ن َّ ي َّوْماً وَّ مَّن ْ صَّزَّف إَّلل َّهُّ عَّن ْهُّ وَّ سْوَّسَّه َّ 
إَّلس  َّي ْطَّان ِ سَّن ْعِي ن َّ ي َّوْماً لَّمْ ي َّعْضِ إَّلل َّهَّ وَّ مَّن ْ لَّمْ ي َّعْضِ إَّلل َّهَّ ذَّج َّلَّ إَّلْح َّن  َّه َّ .  
ومن  ع سل رج لي ه ن عذ ج زوج ه من  إلحمام ق لا ن ا س، وإ ن  لم ي ع سلهما ق لا ن ا س.
hadiths.en:
Abu Al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim {a.s} said:  
"Washing the head with sidr (lote tree leaves) brings sustenance abundantly."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Kafi, Volume 6, p. 504 • Makarim Al-Akhlaq, vol. 1, p. 
61 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 633 • Wasa’il Al-Shia, vol. 2, p. 62 • Wasa’il Al-Shia, vol. 2, p. 63 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 
73, p. 87 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 22, H. 295

hadiths.chapter: 22. THE RITUAL BATH OF JUMU’AH, ENTERING THE BATHHOUSE, ITS ETIQUETTE, AND TEACHINGS ON
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said: "Wash your heads with sidr (lote) 
leaves, for every Muqarrab Angel and every Mursil Prophet has sanctified it. Whoever 
washes their head with sidr leaves, Allah {SWT} will remove the whisperings of Shaytan 
(Satan) from them for seventy days. Whoever is free from the whisperings of Shaytan 
for seventy days will not disobey Allah {SWT}, and whoever does not disobey Allah {SWT} 
will enter Paradise."

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 22, H. 296
reference.chapter: THE RITUAL BATH OF JUMU’AH, ENTERING THE BATHHOUSE, ITS ETIQUETTE, AND TEACHINGS ON

hadiths.chapter: 22. THE RITUAL BATH OF JUMU’AH, ENTERING THE BATHHOUSE, ITS ETIQUETTE, AND TEACHINGS ON
hadiths.en:
Imam Hasan ibn Ali {a.s} came out of the bathhouse, and a man met him and 
said: 
"May your (backside) bathing be good."  
Imam {a.s} said: "O’ fool, what business do you have mentioning the ‘backside’ here ?"  
(The man) replied: "May your bath be pleasant"  
Imam {a.s} said: "And if my bath is pleasant, what benefit is that to me?"  
(The man) replied: "May your warm water be pleasant."  
Imam {a.s} said: "Woe to you! Do you not know that 'Hamim' (hot water) is sweat?"  
(The man) asked Him: “So what should I say?”  
Imam {a.s} said: "Say: 'May what is purified from you be pleasant, and may what is 
pleasant from you be purified.'". 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 125 • Makarim Al-Akhlaq, vol. 1, p. 54 • Bihar Al-Anwar, 
Vol. 73, p. 78 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 22, H. 297
reference.chapter: THE RITUAL BATH OF JUMU’AH, ENTERING THE BATHHOUSE, ITS ETIQUETTE, AND TEACHINGS ON
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE RITUAL BATH OF JUMU’AH, ENTERING THE BATHHOUSE, ITS ETIQUETTE, AND TEACHINGS ON
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٢، ح ٢٩٧

hadiths.chapter: 22. THE RITUAL BATH OF JUMU’AH, ENTERING THE BATHHOUSE, ITS ETIQUETTE, AND TEACHINGS ON
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said: "If your brother says to you after 
you come out of the bath, 'May your bath be pleasant,' then reply (to him) with,  
'May Allah {SWT} grant you well-being.'"  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 125 • Makarim Al-Akhlaq, vol. 1, p. 54 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, 
p. 611 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 2, p. 60 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 73, p. 78

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 22, H. 298
reference.chapter: THE RITUAL BATH OF JUMU’AH, ENTERING THE BATHHOUSE, ITS ETIQUETTE, AND TEACHINGS ON

hadiths.chapter: 22. THE RITUAL BATH OF JUMU’AH, ENTERING THE BATHHOUSE, ITS ETIQUETTE, AND TEACHINGS ON
hadiths.ar:
300   -  وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :   «
إَّلْن ِطْن َّه ِ وَّ ن ِكَّاحُّ إَّلْعَّح ُّور ِ .  
وَّ رُّوِى .
hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {saws} said: "Disease is of three types, and the cure is of 
three types : As for the ailments, they are blood, bile, and phlegm.  
The cure for blood is with cupping, The cure for phlegm is the bathhouse,  
And the cure for bile is walking."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 126 • Makarim Al -Akhlaq, vol. 1, p. 54 • Makarim Al -
Akhlaq, vol. 1, p. 76 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 609 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 26, p. 530 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’a, vol. 2, p. 30 • Al -
Fusul al -Muhimmah, Part 3, p. 17 • Bihar Al -Anwar, V ol. 59, p. 127 • Bihar Al -Anwar, Vol. 73, p. 78 • 
Mustadrak Al-Wasail, Vol. 13, p. 85 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 22, H. 299

hadiths.chapter: 22. THE RITUAL BATH OF JUMU’AH, ENTERING THE BATHHOUSE, ITS ETIQUETTE, AND TEACHINGS ON
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"There are three things that can weaken and potentially destroy the body:  
Eating dried meat excessively,  
Entering the bathhouse on a full stomach,  
And marrying an elderly woman."  
It is also reported: "Intimacy on a full stomach."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 608 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 2, p. 52

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 22, H. 300
